items.text: A brand's positioning is not yours to own.

Adapt what is useful, reject what is useless, and add what is specifically your own

items.text: Ads (and briefs and everything really) only need to be three things – true, distinctive, motivating.

items.text: Advertising is about making people feel stuff about things

items.text: At the end of the day, diagnosis, guiding policy, coherent actions is the best and simplest approach to strategy work.

Here's the challenge with strategy. Answers aren't actually that helpful. Sure, it feels incredible when he cracked the strategic challenge – that moment when all the research insights and market analysis crystallize into a beautiful statement. You can't wait to share it. The dopamine rush is real. I live for that shit but then something strange happens. It falls flat not because it's wrong. The answer might be brilliant. But because the very act of providing an answer close the loop.
Hey advertisements, don't try to be like me make me want to be like you

items.text: I wish I actually got in trouble, real trouble with my work ... such as getting arrested for it. Then I would know I was doing something right.

items.text: Like a flick to the brain.

items.text: Many clients actually don't want insight – although that's exactly what they say they want. Insight is challenging because it forces you to change the way you think, the way you act, the way you feel, and the goals you seek.

items.text: Most geniuses—especially those who lead others—prosper not by deconstructing intricate complexities but by exploiting unrecognized simplicities.

items.text: Most people aren't "in the market" for anything most of the time.

items.text: The goal of marketing is making sure that the message taints the tastelessness of existence, and forces us to pay attention.

items.text: To write the best story you can take out all the best lines

We all know that every human being has basic physical needs: for food, for water, for shelter, for clean air. It turns out that, in the same way, all humans have certain basic psychological needs. We need to feel we belong. We need to feel valued. We need to feel we're good at something. We need to feel we have a secure future.
We are makers of difference.
We can be outrageous to a purpose. That, for me, is great advertising.
We can if we think of it as ________

What are you a student of?
What are you certain of?
What are you taking seriously? Stop it.
What are your hobbies?
What concerns you?
What conventions Must you break? What conventions could you break? What conventions should you NOT break?
What do they secretly wish for?
What do you need?
What impact are you having?

What is currently bringing you hope?

What is the role of advertising?

What's holding you back?

What's your most controversial opinion?
